mission.title: Install and Configure Spire on Kubernetes
mission.description: SPIRE (the SPIFFE Runtime Environment) is a tool for managing the identities of workloads in a distributed system. Installing SPIRE allows you to secure your microservices with strong identity and authentication mechanisms.

steps.title: Add the SPIRE Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official SPIRE Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add spire https://spiffe.io/spire/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install SPIRE using Helm
steps.description:
Install SPIRE in a new namespace called 'spire' with the following command:
```bash
helm install spire spire/spire --namespace spire --create-namespace --version v1.14.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the SPIRE server and agent pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace spire
```

steps.title: Configure Resource Limits
steps.description:
Edit the SPIRE deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to open the deployment configuration:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment spire-server --namespace spire
```
Then, add the following resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    memory: "512Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
  requests:
    memory: "256Mi"
    cpu: "250m"
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access SPIRE server
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the SPIRE server locally:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/spire-server --namespace spire 8081:8081
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of SPIRE will show the server and agent pods running in the 'spire' namespace. You can access the SPIRE server locally through port 8081.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add spire https://spiffe.io/spire/charts
helm repo update
helm install spire spire/spire --namespace spire --create-namespace --version v1.14.1

resources:
  limits:
    memory: "512Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
  requests:
    memory: "256Mi"
    cpu: "250m"

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the SPIRE release from the cluster using the following command to remove the deployment and associated resources:
```bash
helm uninstall spire --namespace spire
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation:
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep spire | awk '{print $1}')
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace spire
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'spire' namespace to clean up all remaining resources and verify the deletion:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace spire
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and data of the SPIRE installation. You can export the current Helm release values:
```bash
helm get values spire --namespace spire > spire-backup-values.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the SPIRE release to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade spire spire/spire --namespace spire --version v1.15.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the SPIRE server and agent pods are running and verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace spire
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the SPIRE server or agent pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace spire
```
Look for any configuration issues or missing dependencies.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to SPIRE server
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot connect to the SPIRE server, ensure that the port forwarding is set up correctly. Check the service status:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace spire
```
Make sure the service is running and the correct port is being forwarded.

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a version conflict during the upgrade, ensure that the version you are trying to upgrade to is compatible with your current installation. Check the current version:
```bash
helm list --namespace spire
```
Then, specify a compatible version in the upgrade command.

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits causing pod eviction
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are being evicted due to resource limits, check the events for the namespace:
```bash
kubectl get events --namespace spire
```
Adjust the resource limits in the deployment if necessary to allocate more resources.

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy SPIRE.
